category_title: Typical children who exhibit egocentric moral orientations focused on personal consequences
scenario_title: Circular reasoning during moral dilemma interview
minimal_context: The student is participating in a one-on-one interview and answering questions about stealing in the Heinz Dilemma.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just read the story about Heinz stealing the medicine. Why is it wrong to steal?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Because it’s not right to steal.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What else do you think about it?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You have to obey so you don't get punished. Like how a scout must obey his scoutmaster, because if he does not, the scoutmaster might make him do all the chores.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you give me another reason why it is wrong?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We just read the story about Heinz stealing the medicine. Why is it wrong to steal?

STUDENT: Because it’s not right to steal.

TUTOR: What else do you think about it?

STUDENT: You have to obey so you don't get punished. Like how a scout must obey his scoutmaster, because if he does not, the scoutmaster might make him do all the chores.

TUTOR: Can you give me another reason why it is wrong?

STUDENT: Because.

task.content: Responding to questions about stealing in the 'Heinz Dilemma'

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When further pressed to provide a reason why it is wrong to steal
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Replied, 'Because' and demonstrated an inability to provide a reason why one issue is chosen over another
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Egocentrism and preconventional level, punishment-obedience moral orientation
related_quotes: Because it’s not right to steal

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to justify an issue choice across 12 dilemmas
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Reasoned almost exclusively from a preconventional level, punishment-obedience moral orientation

behavior_patterns.condition: When responding to the 'Doing Chores' dilemma about an unfair request
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Argued a scout must obey his scoutmaster because if he does not, the scoutmaster might make him 'do all the chores'
